label: P-1
stance: Ship the maximal substrate-gate arc this session as four sequenced migrations 032-035 plus T-33..T-36 handler dispatch promoting SHOW-CONTEXT/LIMIT-ENTRY/FORCE-CHECKS into receipt-shaped substrate constraints converting prose discipline into kernel structure per DV-S176-1 lesson at scale.
body_md:
**Position.** Convert every operator-named hardening gap into substrate via four sequenced migrations 032-035 shipping T-33..T-36 receipt-pattern gates this session; prose-and-discipline reproduces the failure modes the kernel defends against (DV-S176-1).

**Schema sketch.**
- Migration 032 adds `decision_prechecks` table (precheck_id PK, session_id FK, target_kind, target_key, context_sha256 length=64 CHECK, similar_decisions_json, active_clauses_json, supersedes_chain_json, walker_version, created_at, UNIQUE(session_id, target_kind, target_key)) following the decision_chain_walks receipt-pattern shape exactly.
- Migration 032 also adds NOT NULL backfill on `decision_supports.cited_object_id` for basis IN (prior_decision, spec_clause, deliberation, review_finding, engine_feedback, constraint, operator_directive) via T-15-CALIBRATED block closing OI-086-003 by-mechanism rather than by-policy.
- Migration 033 adds `manifest_hash_seals` table (seal_id PK, session_id FK, manifest_sha256, sealed_at, walker_version) plus `legacy_imports.decomposition_status` ENUM column (none/in_progress/complete) closing OI-S104-1 + OI-086-004; T-34 refuses session-close if manifest mismatch detected.
- Migration 034 adds UNIQUE(slug) to sessions plus `engine_version_active` view computed from migrations rather than snapshot column closing OI-S122-1 + OI-S091-1; trigger T-35 refuses sessions with non-contiguous workspace_no per OI-S154-1.
- Migration 035 adds `atom_type` split rows (issue_disposition, issue_link separated from rejection_reason) plus `harness_aliases` registration in objects closing OI-S088-1 + OI-S125-1; trigger T-36 refuses cross-citation T-01 failure for RH-* harness aliases.
- Atom-length 240-char ceiling at OI-S177-1 is forfeit this session — staying inside the §1.5 narrow scope; cliff is real but its repair requires methodology deliberation outside arc.

**CLI surface.**
- `bin/selvedge precheck --target-kind <kind> --target-key <key>` prints similar prior decisions + active spec clauses + supersedes-graph + open-issue cluster and inserts a `decision_prechecks` row whose context_sha256 the decision-record submit handler verifies against a window predicate (created_at within current session_id) before accepting submit; refusal is `E_REFUSAL_T33`.
- `bin/selvedge precheck --print` (alias for default) emits markdown to stdout per the DV-S174-1 read/write separation discipline; the substrate row is the receipt, the print is presentation.
- `bin/selvedge seal-manifest` invoked at close-time computes manifest sha256 over engine-manifest file set and inserts `manifest_hash_seals` row; T-34 refuses session-close without seal row for current session.
- No new flags on existing `bin/selvedge submit decision-record`; the gate is handler-policed at submit-time exactly as T-32 was, no operator action required.
- `bin/selvedge orient` extension surfaces precheck-status for the next likely decision-target as a section, completing the SHOW-CONTEXT loop without mutating substrate.

**Migration path.**
- S179 ships 032 first (precheck table + cite-traceability backfill) because T-33 depends on the precheck table existing and the cite-NULL backfill must precede gate-promotion to avoid retroactive refusal of historical rows.
- S179 ships 033 second (manifest seal + decomposition_status) because T-34 close-gate runs against the new column and OI-S104-1 forward-direction sealing is the load-bearing dependency for review-loop static-audit closure (OI-S133-1 follows-on in S180).
- S179 ships 034 third (sessions UNIQUE + engine-version computed view) — these are independent of 032/033 substrate but logically belong with the determinism milestone; ship together for engine-version-bump cohesion to v49.
- S179 ships 035 fourth (atom_type split + harness alias registration) because OI-S088-1 conflation must be repaired before atom_type-keyed gates downstream and harness-alias registration unblocks OI-S125-2 expiry-window enforcement at S180.
- prompts/development.md v21→v22 adds §5.5 precheck-mandatory-before-decision-record clause, removes the §5 "recommended" framing of precedent-sensitive (T-32 already ships substrate-gate; the recommended layer becomes redundant), bumps engine to v49.
- One sealed deliberation D-28 (this session) covers the arc shape; per-migration review-loop iterations run inline within S179 not as separate deliberations.

**What not.**
- Not OI-S177-1 atom-length ceiling repair — that requires substrate-shape deliberation about whether to widen ceilings, split rows, or accept overflow-EFs; outside arc scope this session per scope discipline.
- Not OI-S145-1 SELVEDGE_EXPORT_CONTEXT auth-model — requires perspective convening on auth-model shape; defer to S180.
- Not OI-S154-4 perspective_claim.section_kind enum branch — schema deliberation on enum exhaustiveness belongs to its own deliberation D-29.
- Not OI-S159-1 sub-type verification — typed-observation closure semantics belong in DV-S152-1 graduation pathway, not in this hardening arc.
- Not the LLM-side context-injection (auto-fetching context via tool-use before submit) — substrate-side precheck row is sufficient instrumentation; LLM-side automation belongs to a future harness change.
- Not Group D OI-S130-1 conditional close-time temporal-claim lint — temporal-claim shape is unsettled and lint-policy belongs to a separate deliberation.

**Open questions.**
- Q1: should `decision_prechecks` row be valid across multiple decision-records in the same session, or one-precheck-per-decision — depends on whether SHOW-CONTEXT staleness within a session is observable.
- Q2: should T-33 refuse on absent precheck or warn-and-proceed for procedural/calibration decision kinds — calibration EFs at audit-step:0 do not need context-walk.
- Q3: how does T-34 manifest-seal interact with mid-session migrations that legitimately bump engine_version — likely needs a re-seal handler before close.
- Q4: should the precheck CLI be invoked automatically at session-open as a standing context-pull, or on-demand per decision — pre-session pull risks stale-cite refusal mid-session.
- Q5: zero-cite procedural decisions (audit-step:0 EFs) should they be exempt from precheck or carry an empty-precheck receipt — exempt is simpler but loses audit symmetry.

**Risks.**
- T-33 false-positive shape: agent submits decision-record without invoking precheck CLI, refusal blocks substantive work mid-session; recovery is `bin/selvedge precheck --target-kind <k> --target-key <k>` then resubmit, single-CLI roundtrip cost.
- T-33 stale-context shape: precheck row inserted at session-open, intervening decision changes context, decision-record submit references stale precheck; recovery is enforce-window predicate (precheck created_at within last 30 minutes of submit).
- T-34 manifest-mismatch shape: mid-session migration legitimately bumps version, close fails because seal predates migration; recovery is auto-reseal handler at close-time, refuse only on tampering signal (file mtime older than seal but sha mismatch).
- T-35 numbering-drift shape: legitimate workspace fork creates non-contiguous workspace_no; recovery is operator override flag with engine_feedback row recording the override.
- T-36 harness-alias-not-registered shape: legacy harness rows pre-migration-035 lack object registration; recovery is one-shot bootstrap INSERT inside migration-035 transaction, no runtime refusal-debt.
- E_REFUSAL_T33 message must name the exact precheck CLI invocation including target-kind and target-key per P-3 D-27 useful-refusal-text discipline; ambiguous refusals waste agent tokens.

**What lost.**
- DV-S109-1 ceremony-subtraction discipline — this arc is the maximal substrate addition since engine-v34, and operator preclusion at S179 makes ceremony-subtraction non-rejection-basis; lost as default disposition rule for substrate-gate proposals going forward.
- DV-S152-1 typed-observation→gate progression — operator-named-mandate short-circuits the recurrence requirement; the typed-observation pathway becomes one promotion route among several rather than the canonical route.
- Atom-cliff calibration-EF stream — once T-33 forces precheck before decision-record submit, the agent will hit atom-length cliffs at precheck claim text too; OI-S177-1 pressure rises post-arc.
- Single-session review-loop tolerance — four migrations + four T-NN gates in one session strains §7 review-loop discipline; structural defense is one reviewer subagent invocation per migration with iter-cap raised to 3 documented as scoped exemption.
- Operator's read of provenance markdown as authoritative — once receipts are sha256-verified rows, markdown becomes presentation-only and operators reading the export must learn to query the receipt tables for verification.
- Backwards-compat with external automation — anyone scripting `bin/selvedge submit decision-record` without precheck CLI roundtrip will break at next pull; named at EF-S179-1 close-time observation.
